questions.content: Explain the differences between SQL and NoSQL databases. Discuss their use cases, advantages, and when you would choose one over the other.
questions.subject: Database Systems

rubric.scoring_guide: Full marks: Covers 4+ key points with clear examples. Partial: 2-3 points covered.

questions.explanation: SQL databases are relational with fixed schemas and ACID compliance, ideal for structured data. NoSQL databases are flexible, schema-less, and horizontally scalable, better for unstructured data and high-volume applications.

questions.content: What is the difference between a process and a thread? Explain with examples and discuss the advantages and disadvantages of multi-threading.
questions.subject: Operating Systems

key_points: Process: independent execution unit with own memory space
Thread: lightweight execution unit within a process
rubric.scoring_guide: Award 4 points per key point covered with clarity.

questions.explanation: Processes are independent with separate memory, while threads share memory within a process. Multi-threading enables parallelism but requires synchronization to avoid race conditions.

questions.content: Describe the OSI model and explain the function of each layer. Why is this layered architecture important?
questions.subject: Computer Networks

key_points: Examples of protocols at each layer
rubric.scoring_guide: 5 points per key point. Deduct for missing layers or incorrect functions.

questions.explanation: OSI has 7 layers: Physical, Data Link, Network, Transport, Session, Presentation, Application. Layering provides modularity, standardization, and easier troubleshooting.

questions.content: What is Object-Oriented Programming (OOP)? Explain the four main principles of OOP with examples.
questions.subject: Software Engineering

key_points: Inheritance: code reuse through parent-child relationships
At least one example for each principle

questions.explanation: OOP organizes code around objects. The four principles are: Encapsulation (data hiding), Abstraction (interface separation), Inheritance (reusability), and Polymorphism (flexibility).

questions.content: Explain what RESTful APIs are and describe the key principles that make an API RESTful. What are the common HTTP methods used in REST?
questions.subject: Web Development

key_points: REST stands for Representational State Transfer
HTTP methods: GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH

questions.explanation: REST is an architectural style for APIs using HTTP. Key principles: statelessness, resource-based URIs, standard HTTP methods, and uniform interface.
